steps.title: Identify the key relationship
steps.content: The strongest clue is Gruen's conception of the mall as “a communal space that encouraged visitors to stroll and linger”—immediately followed by a description of his design with “pedestrian-friendly promenades and ample seating areas.” The design implements the vision.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”Accordingly” signals that the design choices are the natural result of his conception—it means “in a way that is suitable or expected given what came before.” The other three don't fit: “In addition” suggests adding new information, while “Regardless” and “By contrast” wrongly suggest opposition between the vision and design. That matches choice C — C is correct.
